title: Global markets plunge on concerns over AI demand
summary: Asian, European and US stock markets record heavy losses led by the technology sector, with the Nikkei index falling more than 3,500 points.

title: Apple raises MacBook and iPad prices by up to 20% due to memory shortage
summary: The increase, which reaches $200 in some models, responds to the rise in chip costs and affects several of the company's devices.

title: Oil retreats to pre-Iran conflict levels as supply increases
summary: Iraq's pressure to boost production and the normalization of Gulf flows bring crude back to prices not seen since before the war.

title: Bitcoin falls to its lowest level since October 2024 on regulatory uncertainty
summary: The cryptocurrency plummets, dragged down by a 'cocktail of uncertainty' in markets and a negative technical outlook.

title: Micron and Sandisk lead gains on Wall Street after record results from chip company
summary: Expectations of a recovery in the memory sector boost shares of Micron and Sandisk, which record gains of up to 20%.

title: Gold faces bearish technical signals and increased risk of counterfeits
summary: Analysts warn about the increase in fake gold bars on the market and the deterioration of the precious metal's technical outlook.

title: Jamie Dimon prepares his succession at the helm of JPMorgan and appoints two co-presidents
summary: The US bank appoints two senior executives as candidates to replace the legendary CEO, opening a new stage in the leadership race.

title: Binance ceases services in the EU after failing to obtain license, will seek to comply with MiCA from another country
summary: The cryptocurrency exchange confirms its departure from Greece and announces it will apply for the unified European authorization in a different jurisdiction.

title: Honda CEO apologizes for losses in electric vehicles and wins shareholder backing
summary: At the annual meeting, Toshihiro Mibe acknowledges the negative impact of the EV business on results and promises a restructuring.

title: SpaceX plans to launch Starlink mobile services for consumers in the United States
summary: Elon Musk's company prepares a direct offer to the mobile phone market through its satellite network, according to FT reports.

title: SpaceX receives valuation questioned by Morningstar and bubble warning over bond issuance
summary: While Morningstar puts the company's real value at $62 per share, SpaceX's bond sale is labeled a bubble signal by Allianz CIO.

title: Two strong earthquakes of magnitude above 7 shake Venezuela, leaving hundreds trapped
summary: Venezuelan authorities rush to rescue victims of the collapses caused by the twin quakes in several regions of the country.

title: Wall Street closes mixed: Dow Jones hits new highs while Nasdaq falls dragged by Apple
summary: The industrial index rises slightly, but the tech index retreats due to the slump of Apple and Microsoft on a day of mixed results.

title: OpenAI limits initial access to its new GPT-5.6 model and delays IPO until 2027
summary: The company restricts use of its latest artificial intelligence to twenty companies at the request of the US government, and postpones its initial public offering.

title: ANA suffers computer glitch on domestic flights, expects to fix by July
summary: The Japanese airline announces a large-scale system upgrade to correct the failures that affected reservations and operations.

title: Japan renames its Air Self-Defense Forces as 'Aerospace Forces' to bolster satellite surveillance
summary: The approved legal reform transforms the former body into a branch with jurisdiction in outer space.

title: Ryanair eliminates family seat assignment fees after competition authority investigation
summary: The Irish airline changes its seating policy for families after the UK regulator initiated enforcement proceedings.

title: EasyJet rejects £4.9 billion takeover bid and negotiates with Castlelake
summary: The British airline declines the acquisition proposal but begins talks with investment firm Castlelake about possible deals.

title: Court orders TotalEnergies to account for its contribution to greenhouse gas emissions
summary: The ruling requires the French oil company to measure and report the climate impact of all its operations.

title: British construction firms warn of 'earthquake' from doubling of steel tariffs
summary: The increase in taxes on steel imports will raise construction costs and could slow down the real estate sector in the UK.
